description_long: This structure is a tall, lattice-framed tower composed of a network of intersecting metal beams forming a square cross-section. The frame is constructed from slender cylindrical rods, joined at regular intervals to create a repeating triangular truss pattern for stability and strength. At the top, there are four prominent finials or attachment points, likely for supporting electrical equipment or insulators. The open framework allows visibility through the structure, with no solid panels or enclosures present. The surface appears to be metallic, likely galvanized steel, giving it a dark, matte finish. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for supporting electrical infrastructure in urban or suburban environments.
description: A tall, open lattice tower made of intersecting metal rods with a square cross-section and four attachment points at the top. The structure is metallic and utilitarian, designed for supporting electrical equipment.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height and square cross-section, with the lattice truss pattern and four top finials clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizes the narrow profile and reveals the depth of the lattice structure, with diagonal cross-bracing visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, showing the same square cross-section and lattice pattern, with the top attachment points aligned.
description_view_3: Angled side view highlights the three-dimensional geometry of the lattice and the spacing between the vertical rods.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: There are multiple crossarms arranged horizontally at regular intervals along the height, forming a square lattice pattern with diagonal bracing between each level.
attribute_configuration_of_attached_powe: No power lines are visibly attached; the structure appears prepared for future attachment with four top points available for line mounting.
